title: Alekhine's Defense: Four Pawns Attack
summary: Learn the aggressive Four Pawns Attack where White builds a large center then crashes through.
description: Alekhine's Defense is hypermodern: Black lures White into overextending, then attacks the center. You'll learn why the Four Pawns Attack is White's most dangerous response.
line: 1.e4 Nf6 2.e5 Nd5 3.d4 d6 4.c4 Nb6 5.f4 dxe5 6.fxe5 Nc6 7.Nc3 Bg4 8.Nf3 e6 9.Bg5
objectives: Understand the hypermodern idea: let White build, then attack the center.
Learn the Four Pawns Attack: build a big center and crash through.
Master the key idea: the center determines everything.
plans: White builds a pawn center and attacks before Black's pieces coordinate.
Black seeks to undermine the center with ...c5 or ...e5.
The position is sharp: accurate calculation matters.
commonMistakes: Do not overextend. The center is a target.
Avoid slow development. Strike before Black is ready.
Do not underestimate Black's counterplay. The knight is very active.
completionSummary: The Four Pawns Attack creates dangerous positions where White's central control can be decisive.

steps.title: Control the center
steps.coaching: White controls the center. But Black has a surprise.
steps.hint: Push the king pawn two squares.

steps.title: Alekhine's Defense
steps.instruction: The knight attacks e4, inviting White to overextend.
steps.coaching: This is Alekhine's: Black attacks the center with pieces, not pawns.

steps.title: Push and lock the center
steps.instruction: Play e5 to block the knight and claim space.
steps.coaching: White pushes, building a pawn wedge. This is the Four Pawns foundation.

steps.title: Black jumps to the center
steps.instruction: The knight jumps to d5, attacking the e5 pawn.
steps.coaching: This is typical Alekhine: knight jumps into the center to attack the pawn.

steps.title: Build the pawn center
steps.instruction: Play d4 to establish a strong central presence.
steps.coaching: White builds the pawn center. The battle is on!

steps.title: Black prepares development
steps.instruction: Play d6, supporting the center and preparing development.
steps.coaching: Black develops practically. The position is heating up.

steps.title: Expand on the queenside
steps.instruction: Play c4 to control more space.
steps.coaching: White expands queenside while Black deals with the center.

steps.title: Black attacks the pawn
steps.coaching: Black attacks the pawn. White must decide: hold or give?

steps.title: The Four Pawns begin
steps.instruction: Play f4, the defining move of this line.
steps.coaching: This is the Four Pawns Attack! White builds a massive center.

steps.title: Black breaks in the center
steps.coaching: Black breaks the center. The position is now tactical.

steps.title: Recapture and maintain pressure
steps.instruction: Take back, keeping the center intact.
steps.coaching: White maintains the central mass. The attack continues.

steps.title: Black develops the knight
steps.coaching: Black develops. The position is complex and double-edged.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.instruction: Move Nc3, developing toward the center.
steps.coaching: White develops the knight. The center is the key.

steps.title: Black develops the bishop
steps.coaching: Black develops with pressure. The game is heating up.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.coaching: White develops the knight. The attack is building.

steps.title: Black solidifies
steps.instruction: Play e6 to reinforce the center.
steps.coaching: Black solidifies. The position is complex.

steps.title: Develop with pressure
steps.coaching: White develops actively. The attack continues.
